summary.text: React error boundary for the Studio chrome. Exported from

so hosts can also wrap their own subtrees with
the same recoverable behavior.

Catches exceptions generated in descendant components. Unhandled exceptions will cause
the entire component tree to unmount.

. The entry is
intentionally minimal — prompt text and a timestamp, no result
payload, no error record. Failed runs are recorded the same way
as successful ones because the UI typically wants to re-run or
tweak a prompt regardless of how it ended.

Full shape of the export store. Split into read fields (the state
snapshot) and setter actions (the write surface).

Actions live alongside state on the same object rather than under
a nested

sub-key because the surface is small and
consumers read it via selectors. If the store grows past a dozen
fields, consider splitting into context / actions like the
Zustand v5 "split state and actions" pattern.

summary.text: Optional analytics sink. Typed as the runtime-owned

adapter

with lightweight
props only. Omitting it is a complete no-op —

single

AI block. Intentionally carries **no credentials** —
API keys, endpoint URLs, and auth tokens belong to the
host backend, and the AI copilot plugin factory receives
a

callback that wraps whatever network
path the host has set up. Architecture §9 is explicit
about this boundary.

Rendered in place of the editor when the plugin runtime fails to
compile, instead of the component hanging on the loading fallback
forever. Either a static node or a render function receiving the
thrown value. When omitted,

renders a built-in recoverable
error screen (with a Retry action). A successful retry/recompile
clears the error and mounts the editor.

controls). The supported seam for arbitrary host header content — e.g.

. Memoize it host-side so the chrome-props context value

anvilkit chrome, while the lazily-loaded chrome assets resolve).
Lets a host supply its own branded skeleton without wrapping

in a loading-state machine. A supplied node always wins
over the built-in default.

(not a render-prop) so it stays trivially

throws or rejects). Receives the thrown value. Fires once

Optional click handler for the chrome header's Preview action (the
▶ button). Disabled when omitted. Receives the **live** editor document
(read from the Puck API at click time), so the host previews current
edits rather than a stale snapshot — e.g. opening a render route in a new
tab. A
handler stays assignable (the data arg is ignored).

Optional click handler for the panel's "Publish to live" action.
Disabled when omitted. The Puck

pipeline still fires
through Puck's own publish UI; this exists so the AnvilKit chrome
can offer a header button wired to the host's publish flow.

Receives the **live** editor document (read from the Puck API at

arg is simply ignored).

Compose a list of plugin-contributed Puck override slices into a
single

dictionary, with per-key currying so
multiple plugins touching the same key all run.

The input array is consumed in order: earlier entries become
**innermost** wrappers (closer to the default render), later
entries become **outermost**. This matches Puck 0.21's own
internal composition so feeding the result back into

is semantically identical to letting Puck
register each plugin individually — just done once, upfront, so

can also fold in consumer-supplied overrides in the
same pass.
